targets.name: EGFRvIII
targets.full_name: Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor variant III (Glioblastoma)
targets.clinical_relevance: Constitutively active truncated EGFR. No wildtype ligand binding. Known inhibitors: erlotinib, gefitinib, lapatinib. Peptide targeting preferred due to specificity.
targets.notes: Direct extension of Aly's published BBB-penetrant peptide work. Angiopep-2/GE11 fusion candidates available for benchmarking.

targets.name: PD-L1
targets.clinical_relevance: Immune checkpoint. Blocking PD-L1:PD-1 interaction restores T-cell immunity. Combined with radiation therapy shows synergy. Small-molecule inhibitors (atezolizumab, durvalumab) approved; peptide binders could offer better tissue penetration.
targets.notes: Well-characterized, many known binders available for validation. Clinically urgent target for radiation oncology.

targets.name: KRAS_G12C
targets.cancer_prevalence: ~13% of lung cancers, ~3% of pancreatic, ~3% of CRC
targets.clinical_relevance: Most common oncogenic RAS mutation. Historically undruggable — 'you can't drug RAS'. Recent breakthroughs (sotorasib 2021) opened the field. Peptide-based inhibitors could target conformational dynamics that small molecules miss.
targets.notes: Hardest target. Conformationally dynamic. Excellent test of Proteus's ability to explore multi-basin fitness landscape. Publishable if solved.

targets.name: HER2
targets.clinical_relevance: Receptor tyrosine kinase amplified/overexpressed in aggressive breast and gastric cancers. Approved therapeutics: trastuzumab, pertuzumab, T-DM1 (ADC). Peptide binders targeting domain II/IV offer combination therapy opportunities.
targets.notes: Well-validated target with clinical antibodies for benchmarking. Structural data for pertuzumab binding epitope (domain II) available.

targets.name: BCR-ABL1
targets.full_name: BCR-ABL1 Kinase (T315I Gatekeeper Mutation, Drug-Resistant CML)
targets.cancer_prevalence: >95% of CML cases carry BCR-ABL1; T315I in ~15% of imatinib-resistant cases
targets.clinical_relevance: Constitutively active tyrosine kinase driving CML. T315I mutation confers resistance to imatinib, dasatinib, and nilotinib (ponatinib retains activity). Peptide allosteric inhibitors could bypass the gatekeeper resistance mechanism.
targets.notes: T315I gatekeeper mutant — clinically critical resistance target. Allosteric binding sites (myristoyl pocket) accessible to peptides.

targets.name: VEGFR2
targets.cancer_prevalence: Overexpressed in >70% of solid tumors via tumor angiogenesis
targets.clinical_relevance: Master regulator of tumor angiogenesis. VEGF-VEGFR2 axis drives neovascularization in solid tumors. Approved inhibitors: sunitinib, sorafenib, bevacizumab (anti-VEGF). Peptide inhibitors targeting VEGF-binding domain could offer superior selectivity over multi-kinase inhibitors.
literature_references: Ferrara et al. Nat Rev Drug Discov 2004
targets.notes: Anti-angiogenic strategy applicable to most solid tumors. VEGF-mimetic peptides are a validated drug class.

targets.name: AR
targets.full_name: Androgen Receptor Ligand Binding Domain (Prostate Cancer)
targets.cancer_prevalence: AR drives ~90% of prostate cancers; castration-resistant AR mutations in ~30%
targets.clinical_relevance: Androgen receptor drives prostate cancer cell proliferation via ligand binding and nuclear translocation. Standard of care: androgen deprivation therapy, enzalutamide, abiraterone. AR-LBD mutations (W742L, T877A) confer resistance. Peptide antagonists targeting helix-12 surface offer a new resistance-bypass strategy.
targets.notes: Castration-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC) is a major unmet need. Coactivator peptide (FXXLF motif) binding site well-characterized.

targets.name: CTLA-4
targets.full_name: Cytotoxic T-Lymphocyte Antigen 4 (Immune Checkpoint, CD80/CD86 Axis)
targets.cancer_prevalence: CTLA-4 upregulated on tumor-infiltrating T-cells across most cancers
targets.clinical_relevance: Immune checkpoint receptor that inhibits T-cell activation by outcompeting CD28 for B7 binding. Approved antibody: ipilimumab. First checkpoint inhibitor to demonstrate survival benefit in melanoma. Peptide antagonists could offer lower autoimmune toxicity than full antibodies.
targets.notes: CTLA-4/B7-1 interface (3OSK) is the binding target. Structural estimates noted as in-silico. Complement to PD-L1 for combination checkpoint blockade studies.

targets.name: CD19
targets.clinical_relevance: CD19 is the defining surface antigen for B-cell lineage and is universally expressed in B-cell malignancies. Approved CAR-T therapies (axicabtagene ciloleucel, tisagenlecleucel) target CD19. Bispecific antibodies (blinatumomab) also approved. Peptide binders could serve as lightweight targeting arms for ADCs or bispecific formats.
targets.notes: CAR-T target with known antigen loss escape mechanisms. Structural data from FMC63 epitope characterization. Complement CTLA-4/CD19 in lymphoma panel.
